methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Commonwealth Fusion Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Pano AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

data.narrative:
Kraftblock is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Martin Schichtel and Susanne König that has raised €20M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 comparable companies (1 public, 8 private) operating in the same sector and development stage, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach. Across the 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables, valuations range from $120M at the low end to $5.0B, with a median of $355M. This wide dispersion reflects substantial variance in technology maturity, capital intensity, and market perception within the climate tech landscape. Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B represents an outlier, while the tighter cluster of Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M) anchors the central tendency.

The triangulated valuation range is $302M to $408M, with a midpoint of $355M. This single-method analysis carries meaningful uncertainty given the thin peer set and pronounced valuation heterogeneity in climate-tech Series B rounds. Key variables affecting placement within the range include Kraftblock's specific technology stage, capital efficiency relative to peers, and addressable market scope. Substantive upside or downside movement would require additional financial or operational metrics not present in the current dataset.
